title: thelypteris × kumaunica
intro: Thelypteris × kumaunica, a hybrid fern species, is a combination of two parent species, Thelypteris nepalensis and Dryopteris subpinnata, and is native to the Himalayan regions. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Thelypteris × kumaunica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a diuretic, among others. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, decoctions, or infusions, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
